steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank—”With a blend of traditional design elements, such as arched Gothic ceilings, and modern ones, such as floor-to-ceiling windows”—is an introductory prepositional phrase. The right side—”transitional design splits…”—is an independent clause with subject “design” and verb “splits.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for introductory phrases
steps.content: A comma separates an introductory phrase from the independent clause that follows. The other three options violate this requirement—a semicolon demands two independent clauses, a period produces a fragment, and an em-dash marks emphasis or apposition. That matches choice D — D is correct.
